description: Training data mixture specification for SemiticGPT 3B pretraining

he.name: Hebrew
he.rationale: Anchor language — intentionally overrepresented to build strong Hebrew representations that transfer to related Semitic languages (Arabic)

ar.name: Arabic
ar.rationale: Second largest share as primary transfer target. Arabic-Hebrew cross-lingual transfer is a key research hypothesis.

en.name: English
en.rationale: High-quality English data improves overall model quality and enables English-mediated transfer and benchmarking.

fa.name: Farsi (Persian)
fa.rationale: Control language — shares Arabic script but is Indo-European (not Semitic). Tests whether cross-lingual transfer depends on linguistic family vs. script similarity.

shuffling: Document-level shuffle within each language, then interleave according to mixture proportions
deduplication: MinHash-based deduplication within each language corpus
filtering: Length filter (>50 chars), language ID verification, quality heuristics (perplexity-based)
